description_long: This object is a mature coconut with a rough, fibrous brown husk covering its entire surface. The husk displays a dense network of coarse fibers running in various directions, with some areas showing more pronounced linear striations and subtle color variations from dark to lighter brown. One end of the coconut features two distinct, darkened circular depressions, commonly known as 'eyes,' which are natural weak points in the shell. The surface is irregular, with patches of lighter and darker brown, and some areas exhibit small, shallow grooves and natural blemishes. The overall shape is broadly spherical but slightly elongated along one axis, giving it an oval appearance. There are no visible signs of a stem or attachment point, and the coconut appears as a single, standalone fruit.
description: A rough, brown, fibrous coconut with visible 'eyes' and an irregular, oval shape. The surface is covered in dense, coarse fibers and natural markings.
description_view_0: The coconut is seen from above, highlighting the dense, fibrous texture and subtle color variations across the husk.
description_view_1: A side view showing the elongated, oval profile and the linear arrangement of surface fibers.
description_view_2: This view reveals the two prominent 'eyes' or depressions at one end, a distinguishing feature of coconuts.
description_view_3: Another side view with more visible surface blemishes and lighter fiber patches, emphasizing the irregularity of the husk.
attribute_husk_pattern: The husk displays a dense, irregular network of coarse fibers running mostly longitudinally with some crosswise striations. The fibers are unevenly distributed, with some areas showing thicker, more pronounced lines and others appearing smoother. There are natural blemishes and color variations, with patches of lighter and darker brown. The surface is marked by shallow grooves and occasional small, rough spots.
